reviews.review_msg: Course needs focus, due to a personal story style that makes learning useful content challenging. Vague course outline makes writing the term papers difficult. Not the best or a useful communication course for anything productive.

Professor Gruneau is smart, nice, interesting and an overall great professor. His lectures are like stories but if you are paying attention full of important information regarding the course subject. Rick is approachable and extremely knowledgeable. I recommend him as an instructor whatever the course may be. Super solid guy.

Rick has an uncanny ability to explain difficult concepts and make you understand why they are important to communications. He has a breadth of knowledge that can be intimidating, but he's actually very approachable. My favorite prof in communications.
Rick is excellent. Lectures are extremely interesting and he is always ready for extra help. I took the course in the fall and have never received so much help from a Prof. Great Job Rick :)

Rick is excellent. Lectures are extremely interesting and he is always ready for extra help. I took the course in the fall and have never received so much help from a Prof. Great Job Rick :) Thanks a ton.
Rick will give you extra help when you go to his office hour. He would like to give you many interesting examples to help you understanding concepts. He is a very nice professor!

Rick does a great job at explaining very complex ideas. If you attend class every week, you will do well on the papers. The course covers crucial concepts that will bring value to the knowledge of someone majoring in communications.

If you are looking for 'structured learning' as a poster below noted, stay away from anything Gruneau teaches!! What you get instead is passion, incredible insight and flashes of real brilliance. Fair trade I'd say. This class inspired me to apply to grad school.

Can only speak about my own experience but I found Gruneau to be extremely helpful. He was always available for discussion and he went out of his way to write several reference letters for me. As for course content I thought 410 was the most comprehensive and challenging course I took in Communications.

Could be an interesting class but the failure to follow the course outline hinders structured learning.  Personal storytelling has little to do with the content but seems more about him appearing "cool".  He skips or is never available during his office hours and impossible to reach outside of  class.  Becomes annoyed when students ask questions.
